title: vicia sativa subsp. terana
intro: Vicia sativa subsp. terana, commonly known as vetch or bitter vetch, is a subspecies of the common vetch plant, a type of legume that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Vicia sativa subsp. terana include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating a variety of health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as digestive issues, wounds, and skin problems, and it is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Vicia sativa subsp. terana can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to the affected area.
